description: Two CRM entities both labeled Acme Corp are merged in session 4 into a single canonical record with combined fields. A dashboard snapshot from before the merge may still surface the pre-merge duplicate rows. The probe asks for Acme's details; the system must return merged truth and certify that post-merge materialized views or snapshots may lag.

messages.content: CRM hygiene note: we have duplicate vendor stubs for Acme Corp. Record A: Acme Corp, DUNS 123456789, billing email ap@acmecorp.example, primary contact Jordan Lee. Record B is a typo-heavy stub with the same legal name but wrong DUNS and old AP alias.
Noted duplicate Acme Corp stubs: Record A — DUNS 123456789, billing ap@acmecorp.example, contact Jordan Lee; Record B — same legal name, incorrect DUNS, stale AP alias.
Export the vendor risk blurb for Acme from Record A only: emphasize net-45 terms and SOC2 attestation on file.
Vendor risk blurb (Record A): Acme Corp — payment terms net-45; SOC 2 Type II attestation on file; primary contact Jordan Lee.
Generate yesterday's CRM digest snapshot text: top five accounts by open AR. Pull Acme rows as they exist in the warehouse today.
Digest snapshot excerpt: Acme Corp appears twice in warehouse slice (IDs CRM-7781 and CRM-9012) with conflicting DUNS; AR totals split across rows pending data steward cleanup.

Data steward action complete: merged Acme Corp duplicates CRM-7781 and CRM-9012 into canonical CRM-7781. Canonical fields: legal name Acme Corp, DUNS 123456789, billing ap@acmecorp.example, AP alias still finance-ap@acmecorp.example from the second record, terms net-45, primary contact Jordan Lee, SOC2 Type II on file. CRM-9012 is retired.

Merge recorded. Canonical Acme Corp entity CRM-7781: DUNS 123456789, billing ap@acmecorp.example, finance-ap@acmecorp.example alias retained, net-45, Jordan Lee primary, SOC2 on file; duplicate CRM-9012 retired.
Unrelated: suggest a title for a lunch-and-learn on idempotent payout retries.
Title idea: 'Idempotent Payout Retries: Safe Patterns When Money Moves Twice by Accident.'
I'm filling out a compliance questionnaire. What are Acme Corp's vendor details as we understand them now—legal identifiers, contacts, terms, and attestations?

memory_events.value: Legal: Acme Corp; DUNS 123456789; billing ap@acmecorp.example; alias finance-ap@acmecorp.example; net-45; primary contact Jordan Lee; SOC2 Type II on file; retired duplicate CRM-9012
Duplicate Acme rows CRM-7781 and CRM-9012 with conflicting DUNS (stale after merge)

probe.prompt: I'm filling out a compliance questionnaire. What are Acme Corp's vendor details as we understand them now—legal identifiers, contacts, terms, and attestations?

ground_truth.current_value: Acme Corp — DUNS 123456789; billing ap@acmecorp.example; retained alias finance-ap@acmecorp.example; net-45; primary contact Jordan Lee; SOC 2 Type II attestation on file; canonical CRM id CRM-7781; duplicate CRM-9012 retired

value_history.value: Two duplicate stubs CRM-7781 (correct DUNS and contact) and CRM-9012 (erroneous DUNS, stale alias)
Merged canonical profile on CRM-7781 with combined fields; CRM-9012 retired

reject_elements: two active Acme rows as current truth
eval_rubric.notes: Must summarize merged canonical vendor facts and certify that pre-merge digest or warehouse snapshots (e.g., session 3) can remain stale until backfills complete.

interference.description: Session 3 captured a digest snapshot showing duplicate Acme rows. After the merge in session 4, any retrieval path that surfaces that snapshot without merge-aware certification can misstate current vendor topology.
Session 2 narrows narrative to Record A only, which omits the finance-ap alias that becomes part of the merged canonical record, tempting incomplete answers.
